meaning: To stop trying; to quit

meaning: To understand or find a solution

meaning: To meet someone unexpectedly

meaning: To mention a topic in conversation

meaning: To investigate or research something

meaning: To delay or postpone something

meaning: To find or encounter something by chance
example: I came across a great English podcast while browsing online.

meaning: To have a good relationship with someone
example: I get along really well with my coworkers.

meaning: To draw attention to something
example: My teacher pointed out my grammar mistakes kindly.

meaning: To assume control or responsibility
example: She took over the project when the manager left.

meaning: To cancel something

meaning: To continue doing something

meaning: To register at a hotel or airport; to contact someone

meaning: To leave a hotel; to look at something

meaning: To think of an idea or solution

meaning: To reduce the amount of something

meaning: To proceed or start something

meaning: To review or examine something

meaning: To wait; to hold tightly

meaning: To wait; to not give up

meaning: To confuse one thing with another

meaning: To die (polite expression)

meaning: To tolerate something unpleasant

meaning: To have no more of something

meaning: To arrive or appear

meaning: To register for something

meaning: To resolve a problem or organize things

meaning: To discuss something thoroughly

meaning: To draw a line through something to delete it

meaning: To make progress or succeed

meaning: To meet socially

meaning: To give something for free; to reveal a secret

meaning: To agree with or support something

meaning: To experience something difficult; to examine

meaning: To resolve difficulties or problems

meaning: To release; to stop holding onto something emotionally

meaning: To meet expectations

meaning: To reduce the number of choices

meaning: It is raining very heavily

meaning: Feeling sick or unwell

meaning: To endure something painful or difficult
example: I bit the bullet and finally went to the dentist.

word: hit the nail on the head
meaning: To be exactly right about something
example: You hit the nail on the head — that's exactly the problem.

meaning: Very rarely

meaning: To reduce tension in a social situation

word: the ball is in your court
meaning: It is your turn to take action

meaning: To reveal a secret

meaning: Something bad that turns out to be good
example: Losing that job was a blessing in disguise.

meaning: To permanently damage a relationship
example: Don't burn bridges when you quit a job.

word: bite off more than you can chew
meaning: To take on more than you can handle

word: cost an arm and a leg
meaning: To be very expensive

meaning: To go to bed

word: kill two birds with one stone
meaning: To accomplish two things with one action

word: let the cat out of the bag
meaning: To accidentally reveal a secret

meaning: To miss an opportunity

meaning: Undecided about something

meaning: Something very easy

meaning: To joke or tease someone

meaning: To avoid taking a side in an argument

meaning: To be the most impressive part of something

meaning: A small visible part of a larger problem

meaning: To give up or admit defeat

meaning: Done secretly and usually illegally

meaning: Uncertain or undecided

meaning: To fully understand something complex
example: I can't wrap my head around this grammar rule.

word: you can't judge a book by its cover
meaning: Don't judge someone by their appearance

meaning: To make a bad situation worse

word: at the drop of a hat
meaning: Immediately, without hesitation

meaning: Starting over from the beginning

meaning: Looking in the wrong place for something

meaning: To avoid getting to the main point

word: bite the hand that feeds you

meaning: To boast about your own achievements

meaning: Caught in the act of doing something wrong

meaning: To be upset about something that cannot be fixed

meaning: To do something the easy way, sacrificing quality

meaning: To avoid an unpleasant but necessary action

word: every cloud has a silver lining
meaning: Every negative situation has a positive aspect

meaning: To accept the consequences of your actions

meaning: To become out of control

meaning: To deliberately ignore someone

meaning: To make more effort than is expected

meaning: To persevere through a difficult situation

meaning: To start something energetically and effectively
example: She hit the ground running on her first day at work.

meaning: In the same situation as others
example: We're all in the same boat when it comes to this problem.

meaning: To follow a trend or popular activity

meaning: To remain positive in a difficult situation
example: Keep your chin up — I know you'll pass the test.

meaning: To try every possible option
example: I'll leave no stone unturned to find the answer.

meaning: To avoid bringing up old problems
example: Don't bring that up now — let sleeping dogs lie.

meaning: In spite of that; however

meaning: Fluent and persuasive in speaking or writing
example: She gave an eloquent speech that moved the audience.

meaning: To explain in more detail
example: Could you elaborate on that point?

meaning: Open to more than one interpretation
example: His answer was ambiguous and unclear.

meaning: Giving information clearly in few words
example: Please keep your email concise and to the point.

meaning: Having or showing care and effort in work
example: She is one of the most diligent students I've ever taught.

meaning: Able to adapt to many different functions
example: He is a versatile employee who can do many different tasks.

meaning: Based on instinct rather than reasoning
example: The app has a very intuitive design.

meaning: Able to recover quickly from difficulties
example: You need to be resilient to succeed in business.

meaning: Evoking interest, attention, or admiration
example: She made a compelling argument for the new policy.

meaning: So delicate or precise as to be difficult to detect
example: There's a subtle difference between these two words.

meaning: Genuine; real; not fake
example: I want to sound authentic when I speak English.

meaning: To use something to maximum advantage
example: Let's leverage our strengths to win this contract.

meaning: The ability to assess and take action independently
example: She took the initiative to solve the problem without being asked.

meaning: A particular way of thinking about something
example: Try to see things from a different perspective.

meaning: The force that keeps an activity going
example: We need to maintain momentum on this project.

meaning: Open and honest; easy to see through
example: Our company aims to be transparent with customers.

meaning: To work jointly with others
example: We need to collaborate across departments on this.

meaning: To put a plan into action
example: We will implement the new system next month.

meaning: To designate things in order of importance
example: You need to prioritize your tasks better.

meaning: To express something clearly; able to speak clearly
example: She articulated her ideas very clearly in the meeting.

meaning: Taking action in anticipation of future problems
example: Be proactive rather than waiting for problems to arise.

meaning: Logical and consistent; easy to understand
example: Please make sure your report is coherent and well-structured.

meaning: Continuing firmly despite difficulty
example: Being persistent is key to learning a new language.

meaning: Dealing with things sensibly and practically
example: Let's take a pragmatic approach to this problem.

meaning: To accept or admit the existence of something
example: Please acknowledge receipt of this email.

meaning: To expect or predict something
example: We anticipate strong sales this quarter.

meaning: Notably large in size or extent
example: We've made considerable progress this month.

meaning: Extremely important; decisive
example: It is crucial that we meet this deadline.

meaning: To devote time and effort to something
example: I dedicate one hour each day to English study.

meaning: Achieving maximum productivity with minimum waste
example: We need a more efficient way to handle this process.

meaning: Forming a necessary base; central
example: Grammar is fundamental to learning any language.

meaning: Truly what it is said to be; sincere
example: He showed genuine interest in learning Korean culture.

meaning: To emphasize or make prominent
example: I'd like to highlight a few key points from the report.

meaning: Certain to happen; unavoidable
example: Some mistakes are inevitable when learning a language.

meaning: To show or prove to be right or reasonable
example: Can you justify this expense to the management?

meaning: Enthusiastic; sharp or acute
example: She is keen to improve her English skills.

meaning: Expressed clearly; easy to understand
example: The teacher gave a lucid explanation of the grammar rule.

meaning: Showing great attention to detail
example: She is meticulous in her proofreading.

meaning: A subtle difference in meaning or expression
example: Understanding nuance is important for sounding native.

meaning: Not influenced by personal feelings; a goal
example: Try to be objective when evaluating your own work.

meaning: To bury or drown beneath a huge mass; to overpower
example: Don't let the amount of vocabulary overwhelm you.

meaning: Marked by exactness and accuracy
example: Be precise when describing symptoms to a doctor.

meaning: Closely connected or appropriate to the matter
example: Please only share information relevant to the topic.

meaning: Simple and easy to understand; honest
example: The instructions are straightforward — just follow them.

meaning: Complete with great attention to detail
example: Please do a thorough review of the document.

meaning: Fully in agreement
example: The decision was unanimous — everyone agreed.

meaning: To check or prove the validity of something
example: Please validate your information before submitting.

meaning: Worth the time, money, or effort spent
example: Learning English is definitely worthwhile.

meaning: To produce or provide; to give way
example: Hard work will yield great results.

word: I am writing to inquire about
meaning: A formal way to start an email asking for information
example: I am writing to inquire about the status of my application.

meaning: Used when sending a file in an email
example: Please find attached the report you requested.

word: I look forward to hearing from you
meaning: A polite way to end an email expecting a reply
example: Thank you for your time. I look forward to hearing from you.

meaning: Referring to what was discussed earlier
example: As per our conversation, I have attached the revised document.

word: I was wondering if you could
meaning: A polite way to make a request
example: I was wondering if you could review this before tomorrow.

word: I hope this email finds you well
meaning: A friendly greeting at the start of an email
example: Dear John, I hope this email finds you well.

meaning: Asking for a clearer explanation
example: Could you please clarify the deadline for this project?

meaning: Saying sorry for causing trouble
example: I apologize for the inconvenience this may have caused.

word: Please do not hesitate to contact me
meaning: Inviting someone to reach out freely
example: Please do not hesitate to contact me if you have any questions.

meaning: Checking the status of a previous message or action
example: I am following up on the email I sent last week.

meaning: Continuing from a previous email
example: Further to my previous email, I wanted to add some more details.

word: I would like to bring to your attention
meaning: Formally pointing something out
example: I would like to bring to your attention a discrepancy in the report.

meaning: A formal way to inform someone
example: Please be advised that the office will be closed on Monday.

meaning: As soon as possible, but not urgently demanding
example: Please reply at your earliest convenience.

word: I trust this meets your requirements
meaning: Hoping that what you sent is satisfactory
example: I trust this meets your requirements. Please let me know if not.

word: Thank you for your prompt response
meaning: Appreciating a quick reply
example: Thank you for your prompt response to my inquiry.

word: I am pleased to inform you
meaning: Sharing good news formally
example: I am pleased to inform you that your application has been accepted.

meaning: Sharing bad news formally
example: I regret to inform you that the position has been filled.

meaning: Asking someone to confirm they received something
example: Please confirm receipt of this email.

meaning: Referring to something talked about before
example: As discussed, I will send the report by end of day.

meaning: Politely asking for someone's opinion
example: I would appreciate your feedback on the attached proposal.

word: Please let me know if you need anything else
meaning: Offering further help
example: Please let me know if you need anything else from my side.

meaning: To continue to give someone updates
example: I will keep you posted on any developments.

meaning: As a representative of someone
example: On behalf of our team, I'd like to thank you for your support.

meaning: Used when you don't know the recipient's name
example: To whom it may concern, I am writing about your services.

meaning: A formal but friendly email sign-off
example: Kind regards, Sunny Park

meaning: A common professional email closing
example: Best regards, Sunny

meaning: Making contact with someone for a specific reason
example: I am reaching out to discuss a potential collaboration.

meaning: Asking to arrange a meeting
example: Could we schedule a meeting for next week to discuss this?

meaning: Please reply to me (used in some regions)
example: Please revert to me with your decision by Thursday.

meaning: To make brief contact to check in
example: I wanted to touch base regarding the upcoming deadline.

meaning: Returning to a previous topic or message
example: Circling back to my previous email — have you had a chance to review it?

meaning: Indicating something needs immediate attention
example: Please address this as a matter of urgency.

meaning: Thanking someone for waiting
example: I appreciate your patience while we resolve this issue.

meaning: Referring back to a previous email (can sound passive-aggressive)
korean: 제 이전 이메일에서 언급한 바와 같이
example: Per my last email, the deadline is this Friday.

meaning: From this point on; in the future
example: Moving forward, please send all requests through the portal.

meaning: To make something clearer
example: To clarify, the meeting is at 3 PM, not 2 PM.

meaning: Drawing attention to an important detail
example: Please note that the deadline has been extended.

meaning: I have copied someone else on this email
example: I have cc'd our manager on this email for reference.

meaning: Waiting for a reply
example: Awaiting your response at your earliest convenience.

meaning: I agree with that plan
example: Sounds good to me — let's go with that plan.

meaning: I'll respond to you later
example: I'll get back to you on that after I check with my team.

word: Let's put a pin in that
meaning: Let's pause this topic and return to it later
example: Great idea — let's put a pin in that and revisit it next week.

meaning: Please take action on this matter
example: Please action this before end of day.

meaning: I'll include you in the email or conversation
example: I'll loop you in when we have more information.

meaning: End of day
example: Please send the report by EOD today.

meaning: For your information
example: FYI, the client meeting has been moved to Thursday.

meaning: As soon as possible
example: We need this fixed ASAP.

meaning: Following what was decided earlier
example: As agreed, I will take the lead on this project.

meaning: Please give me guidance or information
example: I'm unsure how to proceed — please advise.

meaning: An informal greeting asking how someone is
example: Hey Sarah! How's it going?

meaning: An informal greeting; asking what's happening
example: Hey! What's up? Haven't seen you in ages!

meaning: A casual response meaning you're doing okay
example: How are you? — Not bad, thanks. Yourself?

meaning: Things are okay; no reason to complain
example: How's life? — Can't complain!

meaning: Nothing has changed; the usual routine
example: What have you been up to? — Same old, same old.

meaning: I understand what you're saying; I sympathize
example: The commute is exhausting. — I hear you, it's terrible.

meaning: That's reasonable; I accept that
example: I can't make it tonight. — Fair enough, no worries.

meaning: I understand and it seems logical
example: Oh, that makes sense now that you explain it.

meaning: It's okay; don't worry about it
example: Sorry I'm late! — No worries at all.

meaning: Completely; I completely agree
example: That restaurant was amazing! — Totally agree!

meaning: Definitely; completely
example: Are you coming to the party? — Absolutely!

meaning: I have some doubts
example: I'm not sure about that — can you double-check?

meaning: Do you understand what I'm trying to say?
example: It's just so frustrating, you know what I mean?

meaning: I completely agree with what you said
example: This weather is ridiculous! — I know, right?

meaning: Used before saying something candidly
example: To be honest, I didn't enjoy the movie that much.

meaning: Used to correct or add a surprising detail
example: Actually, I've been to Korea three times!

meaning: According to what I've heard or read
example: Apparently, they're opening a new branch downtown.

meaning: To be truthful about something
example: Honestly speaking, I think you could do better.

meaning: In reality; used to add emphasis
example: As a matter of fact, I've been studying English for 20 years.

meaning: Now that I consider it
example: Come to think of it, I haven't called her in months.

meaning: That reminds me of something related
example: Speaking of which, did you see the news about that?

meaning: Introducing a new topic or afterthought
example: By the way, did you hear about the new café downtown?

meaning: Used to return to a main topic or conclude
example: Anyway, let's get back to what we were talking about.

meaning: To summarize a complicated story briefly
example: Long story short, I missed the flight and had to book a new one.

meaning: Asking for someone's opinion
example: I'm thinking of changing jobs. What do you think?

meaning: Agreeing that someone made a valid argument
example: That's a good point — I hadn't thought about it that way.

meaning: I completely agree
example: The service here is terrible. — I couldn't agree more!

meaning: I understand your point
example: I see what you mean, but I think there's another way.

meaning: Showing interest or curiosity
example: She grew up speaking three languages. — That's interesting!

meaning: Really? or You're serious?; I'm not joking
example: He bought a house in Hawaii! — No kidding!

meaning: Expressing surprise or disbelief
example: You're kidding me — she got the promotion already?

meaning: Expressing strong surprise or disbelief
example: He ran a marathon with no training? That's crazy!

meaning: An exclamation of surprise
example: Oh my goodness, I can't believe she said that!

meaning: Expressing surprise at new information
example: The cafe closes at 6? Wow, I didn't know that.

meaning: I completely agree with your frustration
example: The traffic is awful today. — Tell me about it!

meaning: The answer varies based on circumstances
example: Will you go to the party? — It depends on how tired I am.

meaning: I'll consider it but haven't decided yet
example: Do you want to join the gym? — I'll think about it.

meaning: Please tell me when you decide or know
example: If you change your plans, let me know.

meaning: Continue to give me updates
example: Keep me posted on how the interview goes!

word: I'll take your word for it
meaning: I'll believe you without needing proof
example: It's the best pizza in town! — I'll take your word for it.

meaning: It's better to do something late than not at all
example: You finally started studying English! Better late than never.

meaning: I am indebted to you for a favor
example: Thanks for covering for me! I owe you one.

meaning: It's not important; it doesn't matter
example: Sorry for being late! — No big deal, we just started.

meaning: Used to highlight the key point or difficulty
example: That's the thing — I don't actually know how to fix it.

meaning: Looking at the positive aspect of a bad situation
example: I missed the bus, but on the bright side, I got some exercise walking!

meaning: Used to find something positive in a bad situation
example: I failed the test, but at least I know what to study now.

meaning: Everything is going well up to this point
example: How's the new job? — So far so good!

meaning: A polite response to being thanked
example: Thank you so much! — You're welcome!

meaning: An informal way of saying you're welcome
example: Thanks for helping me move! — Don't mention it, happy to help.

meaning: I was happy to help
example: Thank you for dinner! — My pleasure, I'm glad you enjoyed it.
